notes: Christian Roman elite known for long public service and distinguished virtues, mentioned by Cassiodorus and Ennodius of Pavia.

docs.title: Letter 1005
docs.author: Ennodius of Pavia
docs.excerpt: … as an adornment of his nature, a devoted imitator of his father's perfection, his own industry has made him such a son as another could scarcely have dared to choose. Whatever is finest in the Attic tongue, whatever the Roman tongue has most distinguished, he has mastered.

docs.title: Letter 5017
docs.author: Ennodius of Pavia
docs.excerpt: To Avienus, from Ennodius. Your Greatness does well when you honor both your birth and your character equally. The man who lives up to his origins while adding the luster of personal virtue has achieved what most can only aspire to.
